CDR.title: Back At Plastic (Mr Beatnick Remix) / Into The Dark
CDR.about: Longtime CDR family Om Unit, Mr. Beatnick & Hector Plimmer step up with two tracks dedicated to CDR’s former London home and legendary nightclub Plastic People. Between 2004 and 2014 CDR ran regular sessions at the greatly missed London institution. It was the perfect space for like-minded aspiring and established artists to come together and test their latest productions on a one of a kind soundsystem. Amongst the most regular attendees at the time were Om Unit, Mr. Beatnick, & Hector Plimmer. Mr. Beatnick’s previously unreleased remix of Om Unit’s ‘Back At Plastic’ is an irresistibly swung bottom-heavy excursion, equally hard-bodied as it is ethereal, and dates back to the end of CDR’s time at the club. ‘Into The Dark’ is Plimmer’s brooding homage to the dark dance floor and galvanizing sound of Plastic People, with crisp low slung percussion and undulating synths that build to an intense crescendo.
CDR.credits:
released June 18, 2021
Artwork by Jay Vaz
Mastered by Adam Scrimshire

CDR.title: Create Define Release Vol. 1
CDR.about:
As part of their 20th birthday celebrations, CDR are proud to present Create Define Release Vol. 1. The compilation features a range of artists, all of whom have been regulars at CDR events and part of the wider community over the last two decades. Contributions range from attendees at the original Plastic People sessions in London, like Om Unit, Mr. Beatnick & Hector Plimmer to newcomers like Divalicious K, MJSB and Shunaji, as well as music from CDR's international community, like Johannesburg's X14, Suup Zulu & Lesley Bee, Nairobi's Coco Em, and Tokyo's Daisuke Tanabe, as well as many more.
Musically, the album covers the wide spectrum of music championed by CDR; forward-thinking electronic music of Black origin, whether that be the boundary-shattering club explorations of X14's 'iNtsila', Vida Voyage's ethereal and bass heavy track 'Shallow' or Breakplus' uptempo and Afrobeat-tinged 'Magnetar Flare'. Create Define Release Vol. 1 is but a glimpse into the deep well of talent that can be heard at CDR events the world over.
CDR.credits:
released July 7, 2023
Mastered by Adam Scrimshire
Artwork by Jay Vaz
'Lamba (Version)' features original recordings by Outhouse Ruhabi

CDR.title: Creatrix / Gunga Pea Fonk
CDR.about:
CDR are delighted to announce the relaunch of their label with their first release since 2015 with two tracks from Shunaji & Reginald Omas Mamode IV. Throughout the year we’ll be sharing music by artists from the scenes and cities that CDR has fostered over the years. Each release will aim to highlight a particular era, space or sound that has been a part of CDR over the years.
Shunaji & Reginald Omas Mamode IV have been regulars at CDR’s London sessions for years, having their creations tested out on the sound systems at clubs like Plastic People and Dance Tunnel. Shunaji’s offering, ‘Creatrix’, is driven by an irresistibly syncopated groove that underpins wriggly synths and an elegant plucked melody. Crooner and beatmaker extraordinaire Reginald Omas Mamode IV’s ‘Gunga Pea Fonk’ is a heavy helping of the sound he’s become known for - a dusty, off-kilter beat decked out with squidgy bass and luscious strings.
CDR.credits:
released May 21, 2021
Mastered by Adam Scrimshire
Artwork by Jay Vaz

CDR.title: Sdwadlo / iNtsila
CDR.about:
For our latest release, we turn the spotlight on two of the most promising artists from the extended CDR family in South Africa. Lesley Bee and Suup Zulu were both born and raised in the Alexandra township in Johannesburg and have been steadily making a name for themselves on the local hip-hop scene as both producers and vocalists with a handful of self-released singles and EP's that have captivated audiences in South Africa. X14 (pronounced "zeeah") , originally from Cape Town, has called Johannesburg home for over a decade now. Having quickly immersed himself in the local electronic music scene on his arrival, X14 came into contact with local legend Big Space, who went on to release all of X14's efforts to date on his own Wet Dreams Recordings label.
Lesley Bee & Suup Zulu's 'Sdwadlo' sees both artists trade bars in their native Zulu, celebrating the practice of "swenking" - the working-class Zulu tradtion of young men trying to outdo each other in fashion, choreography and style over all. X14 shows off a totally different side of Johannesburg's musical landscape with ‘iNtsila’. Gritty, bouncing percussion is punctuated with a monstrous distorted synth and spiralling motif, designed with precision for total dancefloor devastation.
CDR.credits:
released October 22, 2021
Artwork by Jay Vaz
Mastered by Adam Scrimshire

CDR.title: mmotd
CDR.label: CDR
CDR.about:
The first season of CDR’s Pathways program closes with ‘mmotd’, a five-track late night odyssey from singer and producer NIIX – complete with a remix by Florentino.
Raised on a musical diet that’s half Kate Bush, half Gatecrasher Anthems CDs, NIIX’s music blends a natural pop sensibility with the sort of dancefloor-focused production that could only come… well, from someone who spends a lot of time in clubs, put it that way. A trained pianist who’s equally inspired by the gauzy modern pop of ML Buch, Ripswirl and Ydegirl as she is the boundary-pushing electronics of Murlo, Sinjin Hawke, Club Romantico and Surusinghe, NIIX’s music sits right in the pocket between the club and the concert hall. Or in other words, she can play ‘Ride on Time’ on the piano.
NIIX self-released her debut EP ‘I’ in 2021, and most recently released a two-track single ‘ocean’ / ‘at bay’ on Safe Space Records, which was supported by Jaguar on BBC Radio 1 Introducing. Live, she’s supported Leon Vynehall, Max Cooper and Jenny Hval, and DJed at Gottwood, We Out Here, Boomtown and Audio Farm. She originally cut her teeth in Manchester where she participated in CDR’s events in the city as both an attendee and a host, but is now based in South London, where she’s a resident DJ at Keep Hush, Subtle Radio and Sister Midnight.
Exploring the three Ls – love, lust and loss – this EP goes big on romance and nostalgic feelings, but is just as effective in the context of a late night alone as it is in the middle of a dancefloor when the light hits just right: a late night cigarette out the bedroom window from one angle; the corner of a packed smoking area from the other. Or in NIIX’s words, “Just as easily consumed alone and feeling reflective, or sweating it out with your pals. Fluid, fun, emotive, unexpected. Just as life should be.”
“I wanted to capture the nostalgic feelings of love, lust and loss as I have always done through my songwriting, but now through the medium of dance music. However, these topics are approached in a less serious way - ‘ghosted’, for example, is a breaksy number literally about being ghosted. ‘mmotd’ (meet me on the dance floor) is euphoric and light, to match the concept of finding your crush in the dance. ‘like a fool’ is the most serious and less club-focussed tune on the EP, and is about falling for the wrong person. Then you have ‘la cantina’, which is based on a makeshift club me and my mates made on a camping trip lol.”
Closing out the EP, we have Club Romantico capitan Florentino’s remix of ‘ghosted’. Forget everything we said previously about lonely smoking areas, this one’s for the front left speaker crew.
CDR.credits:
released October 1, 2025
Written and produced by NIIX
Remix of 'ghosted' by Florentino
Mixed by Tommy Wallwork
Mastered by Adam Scrimshaw
Photography by CICELY GRACE
Artwork by All Purpose Studio
Released by CDR with support from PRS Foundation.
Catalog #: CDR006

CDR.title: 5 Rhythms
CDR.label: CDR
CDR.about:
Following Zar's 'Light Stain at Dawn' EP (early support from DJ Mag, Bandcamp, Resident Advisor, Naina, Anu, Raji Rags, Tom Ravenscroft and more), CDR is excited to announce the second release from their new Pathways programme.
Based in High Wycombe, UK, MJSB has been regularly self-releasing music on Bandcamp since 2020, while also contributing to Tasha’s Neighbourhood label.
Drawing from influences across the UK’s soundsystem lineage as well as dub and minimal techno, MJSB’s music is also a reflection of her personal and spiritual side: in her words, it’s often an outlet to process grief and depression, “an outlet to express difficult emotions through sound … Music has played a massive part in my coping skills, whether that was the soothing effects during an unpredictable childhood, escapism through raving in my twenties, or isolation and focus through creating it in my thirties.”
With an AKAI MPK mini and Maschine MK2 at the heart of her stripped-down set-up, 5 Rhythms is named after Gabrielle Roths’ 5Rhythms model: a dynamic movement practise designed to ignite creativity, connection and community, full. At points stark and icy, at others warm and dubby, this EP’s five tracks put movement firmly at the forefront, and closes with a remix by Detroit techno legend Terrence Dixon. In MJSB’s words, “I’m sure most people can agree that going to the club, dancing and being free in a non-judgemental space feels liberating and harnesses a sense of connection with everybody involved … swimming through the sounds and feeling the vibrations through your body.”
CDR.credits:
released June 6, 2025
Written and produced by MJSB.
Mastered by Adam Scrimshire.
Artwork by All Purpose Studio.
Released by Create Define Release | Catalog #: CDR005

CDR.title: Light Stain At Dawn - EP
CDR.label: CDR
CDR.about:
CDR is proud to present the inaugural release on Pathways, a new groundbreaking artist development programme designed to invest in, nurture and promote the next wave of innovative electronic music creators.
Titled Light Stain at Dawn, the first Pathways release comes from Zar – a thrilling up-and-coming artist whose previous releases have seen him pick up press from Mixmag, DJ Mag & Bandcamp, and scene support from the likes of Naina, Raji Rags, Samrai, Baalti and Suchi. Affiliated with DAYTIMERS and Rhythm Section, this EP sees Zar step out on his own across five tracks focused on the middle ground between functional club music structures and traditional instrumentation.
Ambition is the name of the game here – though it doesn’t take long to realise Zar has the chops to pull it off. Following a short intro, ‘Slip’ sets out his stall: live guitar, bansuri, harmonium & strings working in tandem with a house beat across an eleven-minute epic. The EP’s lead single ‘Deph’ strips things down for the most DJ-friendly track here, before title track ‘Light Stain at Dawn’ lets the live strings stretch out and take centre-stage. Finally, ‘Peace to All Mankind’ closes things on a bittersweet piano loop and chopped samples – proof that for all that the live instruments on this EP soar, sometimes nothing beats the scissors tool.
There’s also a remix by Suchi, whose remix of ‘Deph’ takes the track somewhere subterranean with a growling bassline and staccato keys.
In Zar’s words, “Light Stain at Dawn is the most complete sounding record I've made so far. I’m incredibly excited, happy and proud of what I’ve made, I really hope it resonates with listeners. The record is an exploration of the process of dealing with emotions, from arising through to completion, and the lessons learned along the way. The record was heavily inspired by my experiences of going through therapy. It has many firsts for me: the first time I recorded live instruments, the first time I scored and arranged for a string quartet, the first time I played instruments myself, the first time exploring new genres through contemporary classical. I’m grateful for the opportunity to make this record via CDR’s Pathways programme, thank you for listening.”
CDR.credits:
released March 28, 2025
Written, produced, arranged, recorded & mixed by Arun Dhanjal at Sleepy Sunflower Studios
Additional mixing & production by Tony Nwachukwu at The Old Stationary Building
String Quartet recorded at Sounds Like These Studios, engineering: Charlie Shrives
Track 6 remix produced by Suchi Ahuja & mixed by Works of Intent
Bass Guitar (Tracks 4 & 5) performed by Arun Dhanjal
Bansuri, Acoustic Guitar & Harmonium (Track 2) written & performed by Sameer Khan
Cello (Tracks 1, 2 & 4): Maddy Hamilton
Viola (Tracks 1, 2 & 4): Evie Hilyer-Ziegler
Violin (Tracks 1, 2 & 4): Nina Lim
Violin (Tracks 1, 2 & 4): Kauri May
Mastered by Adam Scrimshire
Artwork: Natasha Patel
A&R: Tom Lea
Special thanks to: Adam Kingsley, Alex Mankoo, Arts Council England, CDR, Charlie Shrives, Charlie Webb, Cherry Yip, Eleanor Howard, Freya Hicks, Genevieve Taylor, Jozef White, Kieran Dhanjal, Natasha Patel, Pritpal Ajimal, PRS Foundation, Rosh Chauhan, Sameer Khan, Sean Keating, Somin Griffin-Dave, Suchi Ahuja, Tom Lea, Tony Nwachukwu, Zak Stone.
No AI was used in the making of this record or artwork.

Titled 'Light Stain at Dawn', the first Pathways release comes from Zar – a thrilling up-and-coming artist whose previous releases have seen him pick up press from Mixmag, DJ Mag & Bandcamp, and scene support from the likes of Naina, Raji Rags, Samrai, Baalti and Suchi.
